originalTitle: Cameroon separatists to pause fighting ahead of Pope visit
title: A Great Pause for Peace in Cameroon!
content:
Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa, ready with some super special news from a wonderful country called Cameroon! Guess what? Something really kind and hopeful is going to happen there. It's about people choosing peace!

Hello from Cameroon!

Cameroon is a beautiful country in Africa. It has sunny beaches, tall mountains, and big forests! Lots of amazing animals like gorillas and chimpanzees live there. Many friendly people call Cameroon their home. Did you know? Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature" because it has so many different landscapes, just like the whole continent!

What's Been Happening?

Sometimes, in different parts of the world, people have disagreements. In some parts of Cameroon, some groups of people have been asking for things to be different. They are called separatists. These groups want to change how their part of the country is run. Sometimes, this has led to fighting. Fighting is very sad. It means people are not always safe, and children cannot always go to school or play outside. We always hope for peace, where everyone can be friends and live happily together.

A Special Visitor is Coming!

Guess who is planning to visit Cameroon soon? It's a very special person called the Pope! The Pope is like a super important leader for many Christians around the world. He travels to different countries to share messages of love, peace, and kindness. He tells everyone to be kind to each other. Did you know? The current Pope is Pope Francis. He wears white robes and always smiles! He loves to meet people, especially children.

Stopping the Fighting for a Good Reason!

Now for the exciting news! The groups of people who have been fighting, the separatists, have made a big decision. They said they will stop fighting for a little while! Yes, that's right – they are going to pause all the fighting before the Pope visits. This is super good news! It means that for a time, things can be calmer and safer for everyone in those areas. This pause is a sign of hope and a step towards a more peaceful time for everyone, especially children.

Why Did They Decide This?

The groups who were fighting said they made this choice because they want to show three very important things. They talked about "responsibility," "restraint," and "respect for human dignity." These are big words, but they mean something very simple and good. "Responsibility" means doing what is right and being thoughtful about others. It's like when you are responsible for your toys and put them away! "Restraint" means stopping yourself from doing something, even if you feel like doing it. It's like when you stop yourself from eating all the cookies so your friend can have some too. And "respect for human dignity" means treating everyone kindly because every person is special and important. It means thinking about how others feel and making sure they are safe and happy. Did you know? Cameroon has two official languages: French and English! That means many people can speak both!

What This Means for Kids!

When fighting stops, it means children can feel safer. They can go to school, play with their friends, and just be kids! This special pause gives everyone a chance to breathe and hope for a better future. The Pope's visit is like a special light of hope. When he comes, he reminds everyone that peace is the best way. He teaches us that listening to each other and being kind can solve many problems.

A Message of Hope and Peace

This news from Cameroon is a wonderful example of people choosing peace, even if it's just for a little while. It shows that even when things are difficult, people can make good choices for everyone. We hope that this pause will help everyone in Cameroon to talk more, listen more, and find ways to live together happily, just like a big family. Did you know? The capital city of Cameroon is called Yaoundé! It's a busy and exciting city. Let's all send our good wishes to the people of Cameroon and to the Pope. May this visit bring lots of joy and a lasting peace to this beautiful African country! Hooray for peace and kindness! Did you know? The Pope is the head of the Catholic Church and lives in a very small country called Vatican City! It's the smallest country in the world! This act of pausing the fighting is a wonderful gift. It shows that people can think of others and make choices that bring smiles instead of sadness. It's a big step towards making the world a kinder place for all of us. Let's remember to always be kind to each other, just like this news reminds us. Every little act of kindness helps make our world a happier place.
summary: Groups fighting in Cameroon have decided to stop for a little while because a special visitor, the Pope, is coming to their country, showing their hope for peace and kindness.
whyMatters: This is important because when fighting stops, children can be safer, go to school, and play, and it gives everyone a chance to hope for more peace and happiness in their country.

originalTitle: Can this man broaden the appeal of a South African party seen by some as 'too white'?
title: Helping Everyone Feel Welcome in South Africa!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're going to talk about something important happening in a wonderful country called South Africa. It's a bit like a big puzzle that grown-ups try to solve, to make sure everyone is happy and safe. Let's learn about it together, in super simple words! Are you ready for a fun story about helping everyone?

Meet a New Leader!

In South Africa, there's a grown-up named Geordin Hill-Lewis. He is a very important person! Geordin has just become the new *leader* of a special team called the Democratic Alliance. You know how a sports team has a captain who helps everyone play fair and win? Well, Geordin is like the captain of this big team of grown-ups. His job is to help his team work for all the people in South Africa. It's a very big and important job!

What is a Political Team?

Now, you might be asking, "What is a 'Democratic Alliance' team?" That's a great question! In every country, grown-ups form groups called *political parties*. Think of them like different teams. Each team has different ideas about how to make the country better. They want to help build good schools, make sure everyone has food, and keep our streets safe. They talk about their ideas, and then grown-ups vote to choose which team they think will do the best job. Geordin's team, the Democratic Alliance, is one of these important teams in South Africa.

The Big Team in South Africa

South Africa is a country bursting with different people! It has so many amazing cultures, languages, and smiles. Everyone is special and important. When it's time for grown-ups to vote, they get to pick which political team they believe will work hard for *all* the people, no matter where they come from, or what language they speak. They want a team that cares about every single child and every single family. It’s like choosing the best team to build the biggest, strongest, and kindest treehouse for everyone to play in! **Did you know?** South Africa has 11 official languages! That's a lot of ways to say "hello"! This shows how many different people live there.

A Big Job for Geordin Hill-Lewis

Geordin, our new leader, has a very big and important job. He knows that his team, the Democratic Alliance, needs to grow even bigger. He said that right now, his team's "support is too narrow." What does "too narrow" mean? Imagine you have a big birthday party, but only kids from your street come. It's fun, but wouldn't it be even more fun if kids from *all* over town came too? Geordin feels a bit like that. He wants his team to have more friends and supporters from *all* over South Africa, not just a few places or a few kinds of people. **Did you know?** When grown-ups vote, they choose leaders who they think will listen to *everyone*, not just a few people.

Making Everyone Feel Welcome

This is where it gets really interesting! Some people in South Africa felt like Geordin's team, the Democratic Alliance, was seen as "too white." This doesn't mean it's bad, but it means that some people felt like maybe not enough different kinds of people were part of that team. It's like if a rainbow only had one colour – it would still be pretty, but not as amazing as a rainbow with *all* the colours! Geordin wants his team to be like a full, colourful rainbow, where everyone feels welcome and important. He wants people from all backgrounds, all colours, and all cultures to feel like they belong and are listened to.

Why It's Important to Have All Kinds of Friends

Why is it so important for a team to have *all kinds* of people? Well, imagine trying to build a really big sandcastle by yourself. It would be hard, right? But if you had friends who were good at digging, friends good at decorating, and friends good at making the water moat, your sandcastle would be the best ever! It's the same for a country. When people from different backgrounds come together, they bring different ideas and different strengths. This makes the team, and in turn, the country, much stronger and smarter! They can solve problems better and make plans that help *everyone*. **Did you know?** When different people work together, they can come up with the best ideas because they see things in many different ways!

His Big Plan!

So, Geordin Hill-Lewis has a super big and exciting plan! He wants to "broaden the appeal" of his team. This means he wants more and more grown-ups, from all parts of South Africa, and from all different kinds of families, to feel good about his team. He wants them to say, "Yes, this team really cares about me and my family!" He plans to do this by listening very carefully to what everyone needs and wants. He wants his team to truly represent what South Africa looks like – a wonderful mix of all kinds of people, working together for a super bright future!

Why This Matters for You!

You might be thinking, "But I'm just a kid, how does this matter to me?" It matters a lot! When leaders like Geordin work hard to make sure *everyone* feels included and important, it makes the whole country a happier and better place. This means better schools for you to learn in, safer parks for you to play in, and more opportunities for your grown-ups to have good jobs. When leaders bring all kinds of people together, they can make decisions that help every single child in the country grow up strong, smart, and full of joy! You can also help by making sure all your friends feel welcome in your games!

Our African Dream!

So, let's cheer for leaders like Geordin Hill-Lewis who want to make sure everyone feels like they belong. Our beautiful Africa is full of amazing people, and when we all work together, like different colours in a magnificent painting, we can create the most wonderful future. Let's always remember to include everyone, listen to different ideas, and help each other shine! That's the African spirit!
summary: Geordin Hill-Lewis, the new leader of a political team in South Africa, wants to make sure his team welcomes and works for all kinds of people, so everyone feels important and included.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders listen to and include everyone, it makes the whole country stronger and better for all families and children, building a happier future for everyone in South Africa.

originalTitle: Benin's Wadagni wins presidency by landslide
title: Benin's Wadagni wins presidency by landslide
content: Electoral commission says Romuald Wadagni has won with over 94% of the vote with most ballots counted.

originalTitle: Why are the US, Iran arguing over duration of uranium enrichment ban?
title: Why are the US, Iran arguing over duration of uranium enrichment ban?
content: The US reportedly wants Iran to stop enriching for 20 years. Iran has agreed to five years. Here's why that matters.

originalTitle: ‘We are a stable, predictable country’: Spanish PM Sanchez in Beijing
title: ‘We are a stable, predictable country’: Spanish PM Sanchez in Beijing
content: Spain's Pedro Sánchez and China's Xi Jinping stressed respect for international law and a shared “sense of justice”.

originalTitle: Is it time we rethink taxes?
title: Helping Hands for Our Homes!
content:
Hello, My Little African Friends!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! I am your friendly news reporter, Amina, and I am so excited to share some wonderful news with you today. I love telling stories about our amazing continent, from the big cities to the quiet villages. Today, we are going to talk about something super important for our homes, our schools, and our playgrounds. It's like a secret ingredient that makes our communities strong and happy! It's a special way we all help each other. Have you ever wondered how we get strong, smooth roads to play near, or clean water flowing from our taps, or bright, colourful classrooms to learn in? Well, there's a very special way we all help make these good things happen. It's a bit like when you share your favourite mango with a friend, or when your family works together to make a delicious jollof rice meal. Everyone helps a little bit, and together, we create something wonderful!

What is "Tax"? Our Community's Helping Hand!

Now, let's talk about a word you might hear grown-ups say: "tax." It sounds like a big, grown-up word, doesn't it? But it's actually quite simple to understand, like a fun game. Think of "tax" as special money that grown-ups pay. It's not just one grown-up, but lots and lots of grown-ups in our country. Every parent, every teacher, every shopkeeper helps. It's like a big community savings pot for everyone. Each grown-up puts a tiny little bit of their money into this pot. And this pot of money helps everyone in the community! Imagine your dad or mum goes to work. They earn money to buy tasty food, comfortable clothes, and maybe even a special toy for you! A small, tiny part of that money also goes into our community's helping hand pot. Did you know? In many African countries, people pay a little bit of tax when they buy things like yummy bread, a new school uniform, or even a fizzy drink. It's a tiny bit added to the price that helps our community.

Why Do Grown-Ups Pay This Special Money?

So, why do grown-ups pay this money? It's for all the good things we need to live happily, safely, and healthily! It's like magic money that builds and fixes so many important things around us. This special community money helps build strong, safe schools, where you learn to read, write, count, and play with your friends. Imagine learning without a sturdy roof over your head or without chalkboards! It also helps build big hospitals and local clinics, where kind doctors and clever nurses can help us when we are sick or hurt. They make sure we get better quickly so we can go back to playing! Did you know? This money also helps keep our streets clean and safe for everyone. It pays for people to collect rubbish and make sure our parks and play areas are nice and tidy for you to enjoy. Think about the water taps in your home or school. This special money helps bring clean, fresh water right to your taps! Clean water is super important for drinking, washing, and staying healthy. And what about electricity? This money can help make sure our lights turn on and our phones charge, so we can connect with family and learn more! Power helps our schools and hospitals work too.

Who Looks After Our Community Money Pot?

That's a super important question! There are special people called the "government" who are in charge of looking after this big community money pot. The government is a group of grown-ups who are chosen to help run our country. They are like the leaders of our big African family. They make important decisions about how to use the community money in the best possible way. They decide if we need more schools in a growing town, or a new, smooth road to connect two villages, or more doctors and medicines in hospitals. Their job is to try and make sure everyone in the country benefits from this money. It's a very big and important job, like being the leader of a very large, diverse family! They want to make sure everyone is safe, happy, and has what they need to grow strong. Did you know? Sometimes, the government also uses this money to help our wonderful farmers grow even more delicious food, like maize, yams, and groundnuts, so everyone has enough to eat! Yummy vegetables and fruits for all our families!

Imagine Our Town Without This Helping Hand!

Let's play a fun but serious game of "imagine." Imagine if grown-ups did not put any money into this special community pot. What do you think would happen then? Our schools might not have enough books, pens, or even strong chairs for everyone. Our classrooms might not be very bright or comfortable, making it hard to learn. The roads might get very bumpy and full of holes, making it hard and dangerous for buses, cars, and even bicycles to travel safely. It would be hard to get to school or the market to buy food! Our hospitals might not have enough medicines to make sick people better, or enough beds for everyone who needs help. Our water taps might not have clean water flowing out. It sounds a bit sad and difficult, doesn't it? That's why this special money, our "tax," is so, so important for our communities to grow and thrive. It truly helps us all live better lives!

"Rethink" Taxes? What Does That Mean For Us?

Now, the grown-ups are sometimes talking about "rethinking" taxes. What does "rethink" mean? It's a clever word! It means to think about something again, maybe in a brand new way, to make it even better and stronger than before! Like when you try to build a really tall tower with your blocks, and it falls over, so you "rethink" how to build it stronger next time. So, when grown-ups talk about rethinking taxes, they are asking very important questions like: "Is there an even better, fairer way to collect this special money from everyone? And is there an even better, smarter way to use this money to help everyone in our country?" Maybe they want to make sure *everyone* who should put money into the pot actually does. And maybe they want to make sure the money in the pot is used very, very carefully, wisely, and without any waste. Did you know? Thinking about how to make things better, like a process or a system, is called "innovation"! Even you can innovate your play or how you organize your toys!

Making Our Community Money Work Even Better!

Rethinking taxes means we want to make sure our community money pot is as full as it can be, fairly. And that every single coin, every single Naira, Cedi, Shilling, or Rand is used for the very best things for all of us. It's like making sure your favourite juice box is completely full and that you don't spill any drops! Every single drop is important and precious. Sometimes, grown-ups talk about making sure richer people pay their fair share. This means if a person or a company has more money, they might put a little more into the community pot, because they can afford to help more. And it also means making sure the money is not wasted at all. We want every school built, every road fixed smoothly, every tap flowing with clean water, and every clinic having the medicines it needs! No money should be lost or used for things that don't help people. Did you know? In many African countries, communities often come together to volunteer their time and effort for projects, like cleaning up local markets or painting school walls. This helps the government too by keeping things neat and cared for!

How Can We All Help? (Yes, Even Kids Like You!)

Even though you don't pay "tax" yet because you are still growing up, you can still help make our communities strong and vibrant! How can you help? By looking after the good things that tax money helps build! Don't throw rubbish in our beautiful parks. Look after your school books, desks, and chairs so they last longer for other children too. Don't waste precious water when you wash your hands or drink. When you take good care of our schools, our roads, our parks, and our water, you are helping the community money last longer and do even more good for more people! You are helping to save money for other important things. And when you learn well in school, you are preparing to be a grown-up who will also help build a strong, bright country. Maybe you will even come up with new, clever ideas for how our community money can work even better!

Our African Future is Bright!

So, you see, "tax" is not just a grown-up word to be afraid of. It's about all of us, working together, helping each other, like a big, happy African family or a busy ant colony building something amazing. It's about sharing and caring, so that every child has a good school, every person has clean water, and every family has safe roads and healthy places to live. When grown-ups "rethink" taxes, they are trying to make our future even brighter and better for everyone. They are looking for new, smart ways to make sure our community money pot works its magic fairly and powerfully for all. Let's all be proud of our communities and help them shine like the brightest star in the African night sky! We are strong, we are kind, and we are building a wonderful future for Africa, together!
summary: Taxes are special money grown-ups pay to help build good things like schools and roads for our communities, and grown-ups are thinking of new ways to make sure this money works even better for everyone.
whyMatters: This is important because when everyone helps, we can have better schools, clean water, and safe places to live and play, making our whole community strong and happy.

title: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
content: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

originalTitle: 'No fear, pure fire' - Arteta defiant on pivotal week
title: 'No fear, pure fire' - Arteta defiant on pivotal week
content: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta wants the club to play with "pure fire" as they prepare for one of their biggest weeks in years.
